diagrams.title: CREATE TABLE Syntax with Constraints
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a CREATE TABLE statement showing the placement and types of constraints (PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, DEFAULT) within the table definition
elements: PRIMARY KEY constraint box (highlighted in blue)
FOREIGN KEY constraint box (highlighted in green)
diagrams.purpose: Shows the complete syntax structure of CREATE TABLE with constraints, helping learners understand where each constraint type fits in the SQL statement

diagrams.title: Foreign Key Relationship Diagram
diagrams.description: Two tables connected by a foreign key constraint, showing referential integrity with parent-child relationship, cascade options, and constraint enforcement
elements: Parent table (e.g., Departments) with PRIMARY KEY
Child table (e.g., Employees) with FOREIGN KEY
Primary key column highlighted in parent table
Foreign key column highlighted in child table
Connection arrow from FK to PK
Referential action labels (ON DELETE CASCADE, ON UPDATE NO ACTION)
diagrams.purpose: Explains how FOREIGN KEY constraints establish relationships between tables and enforce referential integrity

diagrams.title: Constraint Types Classification
diagrams.description: A flowchart or classification diagram showing all constraint types, their purposes, and examples of each constraint in action
diagrams.purpose: Provides a comprehensive overview of all constraint types and their roles in maintaining database integrity
